NKJV.1: My son, pay attention to my wisdom; Lend your ear to my understanding,
NKJV.2: That you may preserve discretion, And your lips may keep knowledge.
NKJV.3: For the lips of an immoral woman drip honey, And her mouth is smoother than oil;
NKJV.4: But in the end she is bitter as wormwood, Sharp as a two-edged sword.
NKJV.5: Her feet go down to death,  Her steps lay hold of hell.
NKJV.6: Lest you ponder her path of life— Her ways are unstable; You do not know them.
NKJV.7: Therefore hear me now, my children, And do not depart from the words of my mouth.
NKJV.8: Remove your way far from her, And do not go near the door of her house,
NKJV.9: Lest you give your honor to others, And your years to the cruel one;
NKJV.10: Lest aliens be filled with your wealth, And your labors go to the house of a foreigner;
NKJV.11: And you mourn at last, When your flesh and your body are consumed,
NKJV.12: And say: “How I have hated instruction, And my heart despised correction!
NKJV.13: I have not obeyed the voice of my teachers, Nor inclined my ear to those who instructed me!
NKJV.14: I was on the verge of total ruin, In the midst of the assembly and congregation.”
NKJV.15: Drink water from your own cistern, And running water from your own well.
NKJV.16: Should your fountains be dispersed abroad, Streams of water in the streets?
NKJV.17: Let them be only your own, And not for strangers with you.
NKJV.18: Let your fountain be blessed, And rejoice with the wife of your youth.
NKJV.19: As a loving deer and a graceful doe, Let her breasts satisfy you at all times; And always be enraptured with her love.
NKJV.20: For why should you, my son, be enraptured by an immoral woman, And be embraced in the arms of a seductress?
NKJV.21: For the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD, And He ponders all his paths.
NKJV.22: His own iniquities entrap the wicked man, And he is caught in the cords of his sin.
NKJV.23: He shall die for lack of instruction, And in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.

KJV.17: Let them be only thine own, and not strangers'<S>2114</S> with thee.

KJV.19: Let her be as the loving<S>158</S> hind<S>365</S> and pleasant<S>2580</S> roe<S>3280</S>; let her breasts<S>1717</S> satisfy<S>7301</S> thee at all times<S>6256</S>; and be thou ravished<S>7686</S> always<S>8548</S> with her love<S>160</S>. <sup>satisfy: Heb. water thee</sup> <sup>be thou: Heb. err thou always in her love</sup>

ESV.1: My son, be attentive to my wisdom; incline your ear to my understanding,
ESV.2: that you may keep discretion, and your lips may guard knowledge.
ESV.3: For the lips of a forbidden  woman drip honey, and her speech  is smoother than oil,
ESV.4: but in the end she is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a two-edged sword.
ESV.5: Her feet go down to death; her steps follow the path to  Sheol;
ESV.6: she does not ponder the path of life; her ways wander, and she does not know it.
ESV.7: And now, O sons, listen to me, and do not depart from the words of my mouth.
ESV.8: Keep your way far from her, and do not go near the door of her house,
ESV.9: lest you give your honor to others and your years to the merciless,
ESV.10: lest strangers take their fill of your strength, and your labors go to the house of a foreigner,
ESV.11: and at the end of your life you groan, when your flesh and body are consumed,
ESV.12: and you say, “How I hated discipline, and my heart despised reproof!
ESV.13: I did not listen to the voice of my teachers or incline my ear to my instructors.
ESV.14: I am at the brink of utter ruin in the assembled congregation.”
ESV.15: Drink water from your own cistern, flowing water from your own well.
ESV.16: Should your springs be scattered abroad, streams of water in the streets?
ESV.17: Let them be for yourself alone, and not for strangers with you.
ESV.18: Let your fountain be blessed, and rejoice in the wife of your youth,
ESV.19: a lovely deer, a graceful doe. Let her breasts fill you at all times with delight; be intoxicated  always in her love.
ESV.20: Why should you be intoxicated, my son, with a forbidden woman and embrace the bosom of an adulteress?
ESV.21: For a man's ways are before the eyes of the LORD, and he ponders  all his paths.
ESV.22: The iniquities of the wicked ensnare him, and he is held fast in the cords of his sin.
ESV.23: He dies for lack of discipline, and because of his great folly he is led astray.

NASB.1: My son, give attention to my wisdom, Incline your ear to my understanding;
NASB.2: That you may observe discretion And your lips may reserve knowledge.
NASB.3: For the lips of an adulteress drip honey And smoother than oil is her speech;
NASB.4: But in the end she is bitter as wormwood, Sharp as a two-edged sword.
NASB.5: Her feet go down to death, Her steps take hold of Sheol.
NASB.6: She does not ponder the path of life; Her ways are unstable, she does not know [it].
NASB.7: Now then, [my] sons, listen to me And do not depart from the words of my mouth.
NASB.8: Keep your way far from her And do not go near the door of her house,
NASB.9: Or you will give your vigor to others And your years to the cruel one;
NASB.10: And strangers will be filled with your strength And your hard-earned goods [will go] to the house of an alien;
NASB.11: And you groan at your final end, When your flesh and your body are consumed;
NASB.12: And you say, 'How I have hated instruction! And my heart spurned reproof!
NASB.13: 'I have not listened to the voice of my teachers, Nor inclined my ear to my instructors!
NASB.14: 'I was almost in utter ruin In the midst of the assembly and congregation.'
NASB.15: Drink water from your own cistern And fresh water from your own well.
NASB.16: Should your springs be dispersed abroad, Streams of water in the streets?
NASB.17: Let them be yours alone And not for strangers with you.
NASB.18: Let your fountain be blessed, And rejoice in the wife of your youth.
NASB.19: [As] a loving hind and a graceful doe, Let her breasts satisfy you at all times; Be exhilarated always with her love.
NASB.20: For why should you, my son, be exhilarated with an adulteress And embrace the bosom of a foreigner?
NASB.21: For the ways of a man are before the eyes of the LORD, And He watches all his paths.
NASB.22: His own iniquities will capture the wicked, And he will be held with the cords of his sin.
NASB.23: He will die for lack of instruction, And in the greatness of his folly he will go astray.

NLT.1:
My son, pay attention to my wisdom;
listen carefully to my wise counsel.
NLT.2:
Then you will show discernment,
and your lips will express what you’ve learned.
NLT.3:
For the lips of an immoral woman are as sweet as honey,
and her mouth is smoother than oil.
NLT.4:
But in the end she is as bitter as poison,
as dangerous as a double-edged sword.
NLT.5:
Her feet go down to death;
her steps lead straight to the grave.
NLT.6:
For she cares nothing about the path to life.
She staggers down a crooked trail and doesn’t realize it.
NLT.7:
So now, my sons, listen to me.
Never stray from what I am about to say:
NLT.8:
Stay away from her!
Don’t go near the door of her house!
NLT.9:
If you do, you will lose your honor
and will lose to merciless people all you have achieved.
NLT.10:
Strangers will consume your wealth,
and someone else will enjoy the fruit of your labor.
NLT.11:
In the end you will groan in anguish
when disease consumes your body.
NLT.12:
You will say, “How I hated discipline!
If only I had not ignored all the warnings!
NLT.13:
Oh, why didn’t I listen to my teachers?
Why didn’t I pay attention to my instructors?
NLT.14:
I have come to the brink of utter ruin,
and now I must face public disgrace.”
NLT.15:
Drink water from your own well —
share your love only with your wife.
NLT.16:
Why spill the water of your springs in the streets,
having sex with just anyone?
NLT.17:
You should reserve it for yourselves.
Never share it with strangers.
NLT.18:
Let your wife be a fountain of blessing for you.
Rejoice in the wife of your youth.
NLT.19:
She is a loving deer, a graceful doe.
Let her breasts satisfy you always.
May you always be captivated by her love.
NLT.20:
Why be captivated, my son, by an immoral woman,
or fondle the breasts of a promiscuous woman?
NLT.21:
For the LORD sees clearly what a man does,
examining every path he takes.
NLT.22:
An evil man is held captive by his own sins;
they are ropes that catch and hold him.
NLT.23:
He will die for lack of self-control;
he will be lost because of his great foolishness.

WEB.1: My son, pay attention to my wisdom. Turn your ear to my understanding:
WEB.2: that you may maintain discretion, that your lips may preserve knowledge.
WEB.3: For the lips of an adulteress drip honey. Her mouth is smoother than oil,
WEB.4: but in the end she is as bitter as wormwood, and as sharp as a two-edged sword.
WEB.5: Her feet go down to death. Her steps lead straight to Sheol.
WEB.6: She gives no thought to the way of life. Her ways are crooked, and she doesn’t know it.
WEB.7: Now therefore, my sons, listen to me. Don’t depart from the words of my mouth.
WEB.8: Remove your way far from her. Don’t come near the door of her house,
WEB.9: lest you give your honor to others, and your years to the cruel one;
WEB.10: lest strangers feast on your wealth, and your labors enrich another man’s house.
WEB.11: You will groan at your latter end, when your flesh and your body are consumed,
WEB.12: and say, “How I have hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof;
WEB.13: neither have I obeyed the voice of my teachers, nor turned my ear to those who instructed me!
WEB.14: I have come to the brink of utter ruin, among the gathered assembly.”
WEB.15: Drink water out of your own cistern, running water out of your own well.
WEB.16: Should your springs overflow in the streets, streams of water in the public squares?
WEB.17: Let them be for yourself alone, not for strangers with you.
WEB.18: Let your spring be blessed. Rejoice in the wife of your youth.
WEB.19: A loving doe and a graceful deer — let her breasts satisfy you at all times. Be captivated always with her love.
WEB.20: For why should you, my son, be captivated with an adulteress? Why embrace the bosom of another?
WEB.21: For the ways of man are before Yahweh’s eyes. He examines all his paths.
WEB.22: The evil deeds of the wicked ensnare him. The cords of his sin hold him firmly.
WEB.23: He will die for lack of instruction. In the greatness of his folly, he will go astray.

CSB17.1:
My son, pay attention to my wisdom;
listen closely [1] to my understanding ⓘ
CSB17.2:
so that you may maintain discretion
and your lips safeguard knowledge. ⓙ
CSB17.3:
Though the lips of the forbidden woman drip honey
and her words are [2] smoother than oil,
CSB17.4:
in the end she’s as bitter as wormwood ⓚ
and as sharp as a double-edged sword. ⓛ
CSB17.5:
Her feet go down to death;
her steps head straight for Sheol. ⓜ
CSB17.6:
She doesn’t consider the path of life;
she doesn’t know that her ways are unstable.
CSB17.7:
So now, sons, listen to me,
and don’t turn away from the words from my mouth. ⓝ
CSB17.8:
Keep your way far from her.
Don’t go near the door of her house. ⓐ
CSB17.9:
Otherwise, you will give up your vitality to others
and your years to someone cruel;
CSB17.10:
strangers will drain your resources,
and your hard-earned pay will end up in a foreigner’s house.
CSB17.11:
At the end of your life, you will lament
when your physical body has been consumed,
CSB17.12:
and you will say, “How I hated discipline,
and how my heart despised correction. ⓑ
CSB17.13:
I didn’t obey my teachers
or listen closely [3] to my instructors.
CSB17.14:
I am on the verge of complete ruin
before the entire community.”
CSB17.15:
Drink water from your own cistern,
water flowing from your own well. ⓒ
CSB17.16:
Should your springs flow in the streets,
streams in the public squares? ⓓ
CSB17.17:
They should be for you alone
and not for you to share with strangers.
CSB17.18:
Let your fountain be blessed,
and take pleasure in the wife of your youth. ⓔ
CSB17.19:
A loving deer, a graceful doe [4]  —
let her breasts always satisfy you; ⓕ

CSB17.20:
Why, my son, would you lose yourself
with a forbidden woman

CSB17.23:
He will die because there is no discipline, ⓛ
and be lost because of his great stupidity.

AMP.1:
My son, be attentive to my wisdom [godly wisdom learned by costly experience],
Incline your ear to my understanding;
AMP.2:
That you may exercise discrimination and discretion (good judgment),
And your lips may reserve knowledge and answer wisely [to temptation].
AMP.3:
For the lips of an immoral woman drip honey [like a honeycomb]
And her speech is smoother than oil;
AMP.4:
But in the end she is bitter like [the extract of] wormwood,
Sharp as a two-edged sword.
AMP.5:
Her feet go down to death;
Her steps take hold of Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead),
AMP.6:
So that she does not think [seriously] about the path of life;
Her ways are aimless and unstable; you cannot know where her path leads.
AMP.7:
Now then, my sons, listen to me
And do not depart from (forget) the words of my mouth.
AMP.8:
Let your way [in life] be far from her,
And do not go near the door of her house [avoid even being near the places of temptation],
AMP.9:
Or you will give your honor to others,
And your years to the cruel one,
AMP.10:
And strangers will be filled with your strength
And your hard-earned wealth will go to the house of a foreigner [who does not know God];
AMP.11:
And you will groan when your life is ending,
When your flesh and your body are consumed;
AMP.12:
And you say, "How I hated instruction and discipline,
And my heart despised correction and reproof!
AMP.13:
"I have not listened to the voice of my teachers,
Nor have I inclined my ear to those who instructed me.
AMP.14:
"I was almost in total ruin
In the midst of the assembly and congregation."
AMP.15:
Drink water from your own cistern [of a pure marriage relationship]
And fresh running water from your own well.
AMP.16:
Should your springs (children) be dispersed,
As streams of water in the streets?
AMP.17:
[Confine yourself to your own wife.] Let your children be yours alone,
And not the children of strangers with you.
AMP.18:
Let your fountain (wife) be blessed [with the rewards of fidelity],
And rejoice in the wife of your youth.
AMP.19:
Let her be as a loving hind and graceful doe,
Let her breasts refresh and satisfy you at all times;
Always be exhilarated and delight in her love.
AMP.20:
Why should you, my son, be exhilarated with an immoral woman
And embrace the bosom of an outsider (pagan)?
AMP.21:
For the ways of man are directly before the eyes of the LORD,
And He carefully watches all of his paths [all of his comings and goings].
AMP.22:
The iniquities done by a wicked man will trap him,
And he will be held with the cords of his sin.
AMP.23:
He will die for lack of instruction (discipline),
And in the greatness of his foolishness he will go astray and be lost.

MSG.1:
Dear friend, pay close attention to this, my wisdom;
listen very closely to the way I see it.
MSG.2:
Then you'll acquire a taste for good sense;
what I tell you will keep you out of trouble.
MSG.3:
The lips of a seductive woman are oh so sweet,
her soft words are oh so smooth.
MSG.4:
But it won't be long before she's gravel in your mouth,
a pain in your gut, a wound in your heart.
MSG.5:
She's dancing down the primrose path to Death;
she's headed straight for Hell and taking you with her.
MSG.6:
She hasn't a clue about Real Life,
about who she is or where she's going.
MSG.7:
So, my friend, listen closely;
don't treat my words casually.
MSG.8:
Keep your distance from such a woman;
absolutely stay out of her neighborhood.
MSG.9:
You don't want to squander your wonderful life,
to waste your precious life among the hardhearted.
MSG.10:
Why should you allow strangers to take advantage of you?
Why be exploited by those who care nothing for you?
MSG.11:
You don't want to end your life full of regrets,
nothing but sin and bones,
MSG.12:
Saying, "Oh, why didn't I do what they told me?
Why did I reject a disciplined life?
MSG.13:
Why didn't I listen to my mentors,
or take my teachers seriously?
MSG.14:
My life is ruined!
I haven't one blessed thing to show for my life!"
MSG.15:
Do you know the saying, "Drink from your own rain barrel,
draw water from your own spring-fed well"?
MSG.16:
It's true. Otherwise, you may one day come home
and find your barrel empty and your well polluted.
MSG.17:
Your spring water is for you and you only,
not to be passed around among strangers.
MSG.18:
Bless your fresh-flowing fountain!
Enjoy the wife you married as a young man!
MSG.19:
Lovely as an angel, beautiful as a rose—
don't ever quit taking delight in her body.
Never take her love for granted!
MSG.20:
Why would you trade enduring intimacies for cheap thrills with a whore?
for dalliance with a promiscuous stranger?
MSG.21:
Mark well that God doesn't miss a move you make;
he's aware of every step you take.
MSG.22:
The shadow of your sin will overtake you;
you'll find yourself stumbling all over yourself in the dark.
MSG.23:
Death is the reward of an undisciplined life;
your foolish decisions trap you in a dead end.

NIV.1:
Warning Against Adultery
My son, pay attention to my wisdom,
listen well to my words of insight,
NIV.2:
that you may maintain discretion
and your lips may preserve knowledge.
NIV.3:
For the lips of an adulteress drip honey,
and her speech is smoother than oil;
NIV.4:
but in the end she is bitter as gall,
sharp as a double-edged sword.
NIV.5:
Her feet go down to death;
her steps lead straight to the grave.
NIV.6:
She gives no thought to the way of life;
her paths are crooked, but she knows it not.
NIV.7:
Now then, my sons, listen to me;
do not turn aside from what I say.
NIV.8:
Keep to a path far from her,
do not go near the door of her house,
NIV.9:
lest you give your best strength to others
and your years to one who is cruel,
NIV.10:
lest strangers feast on your wealth
and your toil enrich another man’s house.
NIV.11:
At the end of your life you will groan,
when your flesh and body are spent.
NIV.12:
You will say, “How I hated discipline!
How my heart spurned correction!
NIV.13:
I would not obey my teachers
or listen to my instructors.
NIV.14:
I have come to the brink of utter ruin
in the midst of the whole assembly.”
NIV.15:
Drink water from your own cistern,
running water from your own well.
NIV.16:
Should your springs overflow in the streets,
your streams of water in the public squares?
NIV.17:
Let them be yours alone,
never to be shared with strangers.
NIV.18:
May your fountain be blessed,
and may you rejoice in the wife of your youth.
NIV.19:
A loving doe, a graceful deer—
may her breasts satisfy you always,
may you ever be captivated by her love.
NIV.20:
Why be captivated, my son, by an adulteress?
Why embrace the bosom of another man’s wife?
NIV.21:
For a man’s ways are in full view of the Lord,
and he examines all his paths.
NIV.22:
The evil deeds of a wicked man ensnare him;
the cords of his sin hold him fast.
NIV.23:
He will die for lack of discipline,
led astray by his own great folly.

NRSVCE.1:
My child, be attentive to my wisdom;
incline your ear to my understanding,
NRSVCE.2:
so that you may hold on to prudence,
and your lips may guard knowledge.
NRSVCE.3:
For the lips of a loose woman drip honey,
and her speech is smoother than oil;
NRSVCE.4:
but in the end she is bitter as wormwood,
sharp as a two-edged sword.
NRSVCE.5:
Her feet go down to death;
her steps follow the path to Sheol.
NRSVCE.6:
She does not keep straight to the path of life;
her ways wander, and she does not know it.
NRSVCE.7:
And now, my child, listen to me,
and do not depart from the words of my mouth.
NRSVCE.8:
Keep your way far from her,
and do not go near the door of her house;
NRSVCE.9:
or you will give your honor to others,
and your years to the merciless,
NRSVCE.10:
and strangers will take their fill of your wealth,
and your labors will go to the house of an alien;
NRSVCE.11:
and at the end of your life you will groan,
when your flesh and body are consumed,
NRSVCE.12:
and you say, “Oh, how I hated discipline,
and my heart despised reproof!
NRSVCE.13:
I did not listen to the voice of my teachers
or incline my ear to my instructors.
NRSVCE.14:
Now I am at the point of utter ruin
in the public assembly.”
NRSVCE.15:
Drink water from your own cistern,
flowing water from your own well.
NRSVCE.16:
Should your springs be scattered abroad,
streams of water in the streets?
NRSVCE.17:
Let them be for yourself alone,
and not for sharing with strangers.
NRSVCE.18:
Let your fountain be blessed,
and rejoice in the wife of your youth,
NRSVCE.19:
a lovely deer, a graceful doe.
May her breasts satisfy you at all times;
may you be intoxicated always by her love.
NRSVCE.20:
Why should you be intoxicated, my son, by another woman
and embrace the bosom of an adulteress?
NRSVCE.21:
For human ways are under the eyes of the LORD,
and he examines all their paths.
NRSVCE.22:
The iniquities of the wicked ensnare them,
and they are caught in the toils of their sin.
NRSVCE.23:
They die for lack of discipline,
and because of their great folly they are lost.

NET.1: My child, be attentive to my wisdom, pay close attention to my understanding,
NET.2: in order to safeguard discretion, and that your lips may guard knowledge.
NET.3: For the lips of the adulterous woman drip honey, and her seductive words are smoother than olive oil,
NET.4: but in the end she is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a two-edged sword.
NET.5: Her feet go down to death; her steps lead straight to the grave.
NET.6: Lest she should make level the path leading to life, her paths are unstable but she does not know it.
NET.7: So now, children, listen to me; do not turn aside from the words I speak.
NET.8: Keep yourself far from her, and do not go near the door of her house,
NET.9: lest you give your vigor to others and your years to a cruel person,
NET.10: lest strangers devour your strength, and your labor benefit another man’s house.
NET.11: And at the end of your life you will groan when your flesh and your body are wasted away.
NET.12: And you will say, “How I hated discipline! My heart spurned reproof!
NET.13: For I did not obey my teachers and I did not heed my instructors.
NET.14: I almost came to complete ruin in the midst of the whole congregation!”
NET.15: Drink water from your own cistern and running water from your own well.
NET.16: Should your springs be dispersed outside, your streams of water in the wide plazas?
NET.17: Let them be for yourself alone, and not for strangers with you.
NET.18: May your fountain be blessed, and may you rejoice in your young wife —
NET.19: a loving doe, a graceful deer; may her breasts satisfy you at all times, may you be captivated by her love always.
NET.20: But why should you be captivated, my son, by an adulteress, and embrace the bosom of a different woman?
NET.21: For the ways of a person are in front of the LORD’s eyes, and the LORD weighs all that person’s paths.
NET.22: The wicked will be captured by his own iniquities, and he will be held by the cords of his own sin.
NET.23: He will die because there was no discipline; because of the greatness of his folly he will reel.
